title: The public garden in the morning
summary: Describe a public garden or green space in the morning, using present simple and 'there is / there are'.
topic: A public garden or green space in the morning
prompt: Write 6–8 short sentences about a public garden, square, or park you know. Describe what it looks like in the morning and who you see there. Use present simple, 'there is / there are', and at least one adverb of frequency.
hintStarters: In the morning, there are usually…
I sometimes walk through it when…
sampleText: There is a small public garden two streets from my apartment in Buenos Aires. It has an old stone fountain in the middle and four paths that go out to the corners. In the morning, the grass is always wet with dew. There are usually a few joggers who run in circles on the path. Sometimes an older man does stretching exercises near the iron gate. He is always there at seven o'clock. There are two flower beds near the fountain. They have red and white flowers. Many pigeons and some sparrows walk on the grass and look for food. There is always one bench in the sun and one bench in the shade. I usually sit on the bench in the shade because the morning sun is already strong. I use the garden as a shortcut to the bus stop. I walk through it every day and it always makes me feel calm.

sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: cách căn hộ tôi hai con phố
đài phun nước bằng đá cổ
đường tắt đến trạm xe buýt
khiến tôi cảm thấy bình tĩnh

sampleGrammarNotes.title: 'There is' and 'there are' + noun to describe what exists in a place
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyVi: Dùng 'there is' cho danh từ số ít và không đếm được: 'There is a small garden', 'There is one bench'. Dùng 'there are' cho danh từ số nhiều: 'There are two flower beds', 'There are usually a few joggers'. Cấu trúc này thường dùng khi miêu tả cảnh vật.
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyEn: Use 'there is' for singular and uncountable nouns: 'There is a fountain'. Use 'there are' for plural nouns: 'There are two flower beds'. This structure is common in place descriptions.

sampleTranslationVi: Có một khu vườn công cộng nhỏ cách căn hộ tôi hai con phố ở Buenos Aires. Vườn có một đài phun nước bằng đá cổ ở giữa và bốn con đường dẫn ra bốn góc. Vào buổi sáng, cỏ luôn ướt đẫm sương mai. Thường có một vài người chạy bộ chạy vòng tròn trên đường. Đôi khi một người đàn ông lớn tuổi tập các bài tập giãn cơ gần cổng sắt. Ông ấy luôn ở đó lúc bảy giờ. Có hai luống hoa gần đài phun nước. Chúng có hoa đỏ và trắng. Nhiều chim bồ câu và một vài con chim sẻ đi trên cỏ và tìm kiếm thức ăn. Luôn có một cái ghế trong nắng và một cái ghế trong bóng mát. Tôi thường ngồi ở ghế trong bóng mát vì nắng sáng đã rất mạnh. Tôi dùng khu vườn như một đường tắt đến trạm xe buýt. Tôi đi qua đó mỗi ngày và nó luôn khiến tôi cảm thấy bình tĩnh.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence uses 'there is' correctly for a singular noun?
options: There is a fountain in the middle of the garden.
There are a fountain in the middle of the garden.
There is fountains in the middle of the garden.
There is a fountain in middle garden the.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'a fountain' is singular — use 'there is', not 'there are'.
mcQuestions.hint: Is the noun singular or plural?

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence uses 'there are' correctly for a plural noun?
options: There is two flower beds near the fountain.
There are two flower beds near the fountain.
There are two flower bed near fountain.
There two flower beds are near fountain.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'two flower beds' is plural — use 'there are'; the article 'the' is needed before 'fountain'.
mcQuestions.hint: Plural nouns use 'there are', not 'there is'.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence correctly uses an adverb of frequency with the verb 'be'?
options: The grass always is wet in the morning.
Always the grass wet is in the morning.
The grass is always wet in the morning.
The grass is wet always in morning.
mcQuestions.explanation: With 'be', the adverb of frequency comes after the verb: 'is always wet'.
mcQuestions.hint: Where does the adverb go when the verb is 'be'?

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence correctly says why you sit in the shade?
options: I sit in shade because morning sun strong.
Because the sun strong I sit in the shade.
I sit because shade the morning sun strong.
I sit in the shade because the morning sun is strong.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'because' + subject + verb + adjective: 'because the morning sun is strong'.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence correctly describes birds looking for food?
options: Pigeons walk on the grass and look for food.
Pigeons walks on the grass and looks for food.
Pigeons walk on grass and looking for food.
Pigeons on grass walk and for food look.
mcQuestions.explanation: Plural subject (pigeons) takes no -s: 'walk' and 'look' — consistent present simple.
mcQuestions.hint: Do plural subjects take -s on the verb in present simple?

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence correctly says you use a garden as a shortcut?
options: I use the garden as shortcut bus stop.
I use the garden as a shortcut to the bus stop.
I use garden like a shortcut to the bus stop.
I using the garden as a shortcut to bus stop.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'use X as Y' is the standard structure; 'to the bus stop' needs the article 'the'.
mcQuestions.hint: Which preposition connects 'use' and its purpose?

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence correctly says that someone is always in a specific place at a specific time?
options: He always is there at seven o'clock.
He is there always at seven o'clock.
He is always there at seven o'clock.
Always at seven he there is.
mcQuestions.explanation: With 'be', the adverb of frequency goes right after the verb: 'is always there'.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence correctly says a place makes you feel a certain way?
options: It always makes me to feel calm.
It always makes I feel calm.
It always make me feel calm.
It always makes me feel calm.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'makes me feel calm' — 'make + object + bare infinitive', third-person singular adds -s.
mcQuestions.hint: After 'makes me', does the next verb need 'to' or is it a bare infinitive?

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the best sentence to describe a morning scene in a garden.
options: In the morning, there are usually a few joggers on the path.
In the morning, there is usually a few joggers on the path.
In the morning, there are usually a few jogger on path.
Morning in there are few joggers usually path.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'a few joggers' is plural — use 'there are'; 'usually' before the noun phrase.
mcQuestions.hint: Is 'a few joggers' singular or plural?

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence correctly uses 'and' to describe two things that are true about a garden?
options: It has a fountain and having four paths.
It has a fountain and four paths.
It have a fountain and four paths.
A fountain and four paths it haves.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'It has' is correct third-person singular; 'and' joins two noun objects of the same verb.
mcQuestions.hint: Check the verb ending with 'it', and make sure 'and' joins two similar things.

criticalThinkingQuestion: Is there a green space in your city that you walk through regularly? What would be different about your morning if it disappeared?
